title: trisetum alpestre subsp. glabrescens
intro: Trisetum alpestre subsp. glabrescens is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae, native to mountainous regions in Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant has also been found to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for immune system support and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Trisetum alpestre subsp. glabrescens can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
